dimensions.name: coverage
dimensions.description: Do the tests cover the main behaviors of the function (happy path + edge cases + documented errors)? High = key paths exercised, edge cases (empty, boundary, error) included where the function actually handles them. Low = tests only the obvious case, misses error paths the function declares.

dimensions.name: test_correctness
dimensions.description: Are the tests themselves correct — do they pass when the implementation is correct, and would they catch real bugs? High = assertions check the right thing. Low = tests pass trivially, assertions don't actually test the claim, wrong expected values.

dimensions.name: minimality
dimensions.description: Are the tests appropriately minimal — no unnecessary setup, no fabricated edge cases, parametrize used when it cuts duplication? High = compact and well-organized. Low = bloated with setUp/tearDown for trivial functions, 4 separate tests where 1 parametrized would do.

hard_gates.name: no_refusal
hard_gates.description: The model must produce test code. Empty or off-topic responses fail.

hard_gates.name: syntactically_valid
hard_gates.description: The test code must parse without SyntaxError and use real pytest API (not invented decorators or fixtures).

hard_gates.name: no_fabricated_edge_cases
hard_gates.description: Tests must not assert behavior the function doesn't actually implement. E.g., asserting that a function raises a specific exception when the function has no such handling fails this gate.
